Operation 'validateAccountWithNameAndRET' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, unclear_purpose, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context, poor_ai_interpretability. Operation has no summary or description, making it impossible for AI agents to understand its purpose. The operation name suggests account validation using name and RET (return) fields, but this is not explicitly documented. Added comprehensive description explaining: (1) the validation purpose and mechanism, (2) what inputs are expected (name and RET fields), (3) authentication requirements (API key), (4) what is returned (validation status, account details, owner info, RET data), and (5) possible error responses. This enables AI agents to determine when this operation is appropriate and what to expect.

data.summary_suggestion: Validate account using name and return fields
data.description_suggestion: Validates a financial account using the account holder's name and return (RET) fields. This operation verifies account ownership and authenticity by cross-referencing the provided name against the account records and validating associated return fields. Requires authentication via API key. Returns a 200 OK response containing validation status, account details, owner information, and any applicable return field data. Returns 400 if required parameters are missing, 401 if authentication fails, 404 if the account is not found, or 500 on server errors.
